response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer whose career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows required. He has no demonstrable fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge, and the one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was a general fintech startup focused on retail investment, not institutional capital markets. SQL is listed as a skill but there is no evidence of deep SQL Server or Oracle work; his primary data store experience is MongoDB. The gap between his profile and the mandatory requirements is too wide to bridge with optional strengths.
strengths: Solid CI/CD, DevOps, and trunk-based development practices — aligns with desirable pipeline knowledge
Demonstrated communication skills through public workshops, conference talks, and teaching
Exposure to high-performance, low-latency systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec), showing systems-level thinking
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months at NI + thesis) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge; single fintech role was retail investment tooling
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth; primary database experience is MongoDB and general SQL

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
